Defines the built-in methods and properties for the ECMA Date.prototype object.

# Purpose
The code defines the `Date.prototype` built-in object for a JavaScript engine, specifically focusing on the implementation of its methods and properties. It uses macros to declare object identifiers, object values, and routines that correspond to the methods available on the `Date.prototype`. These methods include various string conversion functions (`toString`, `toDateString`, `toTimeString`, etc.), as well as methods for getting and setting date and time components (such as `getFullYear`, `getMonth`, `setDate`, `setFullYear`, etc.). The code also includes locale-specific and UTC-specific versions of these methods.

The file is structured to allow conditional compilation, as seen with the `CONFIG_DISABLE_ANNEXB_BUILTIN` preprocessor directive, which controls the inclusion of additional methods like `getYear` and `setYear`. This suggests that the code is part of a larger system where certain features can be enabled or disabled based on configuration. The use of macros like `OBJECT_ID`, `OBJECT_VALUE`, and `ROUTINE` indicates that this file is intended to be included in other parts of the system, where these macros are defined to generate the necessary code for the JavaScript engine. The file does not define public APIs directly but rather contributes to the internal implementation of the `Date.prototype` object.
